response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong craftsmanship, low-latency microservices, and some C/C++ background, but the role is a Principal hardware Performance Architect requiring SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design. None of these core competencies appear in the resume; the experience is almost entirely web/mobile/backend software. The educational path (BS + ~14 years) is technically met, but the domain mismatch is fundamental and disqualifying for a Principal-level IC in this specialty.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Some C/C++ systems experience at National Instruments and embedded/robotics work
Broad, senior-level engineering breadth with strong testing/TDD discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
No deep-learning training/inference workload optimization experience
